    • County-level homeless estimates

CoC geographic boundaries, 2007-2025

Source: R/coc.R
coc.Rd

HUD Continuum of Care (CoC) boundary polygons by year, coc2007 through coc2024, as national simple-feature (sf) objects with one row per CoC. Built from HUD's per-state CoC GIS shapefiles, combined nationally, with CoC codes harmonized across vintages (an embedded fiscal year such as "AL07-500" is normalized to "AL-500"). Geometries are topology-preserving simplified (about 2 percent of vertices retained) to keep the package a manageable size; they are intended for national-scale mapping and areal analysis rather than fine local cartography.

coc2007

coc2008

coc2009

coc2010

coc2011

coc2012

coc2013

coc2014

coc2015

coc2016

coc2017

coc2018

coc2019

coc2020

coc2021

coc2022

coc2023

coc2024

coc2025

Format

An sf data frame (CRS EPSG:4269, NAD83) with one row per CoC and 4 attributes plus geometry:

COCNUM

CoC number, e.g. "WA-500".

COCNAME

CoC name.

ST

Two-letter state/territory abbreviation.

STATE_NAME

State/territory name.

geometry

CoC boundary polygon.

An object of class sf (inherits from tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) with 448 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 450 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 442 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 431 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 411 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 397 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) with 393 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 398 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 389 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) with 389 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 386 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from data.frame) with 387 rows and 5 columns.

An object of class sf (inherits from tbl_df, tbl, data.frame) with 388 rows and 5 columns.

Source

HUD CoC GIS tools: https://www.hudexchange.info/programs/coc/gis-tools/
